text:
Market today:
The mood is cautious with geopolitical tensions rising and chip stocks taking a hit after Samsung missed AI expectations. This directly impacts your tech/chip heavy portfolio, especially with leveraged positions.

Your holdings:
SOXL (-34.96%) is a major concern with its "SELL" status and leveraged decay. SPAL (-20.22%), also leveraged, is below its stop and needs attention. Remember, margin amplifies these drawdowns significantly.

Watch:
Keep an eye on geopolitical developments and any further news on chip sector performance. The "cyber stock comeback" narrative could provide a tailwind for names like ZS.

Bottom line:
Prioritize risk management and address your highly leveraged, underperforming positions immediately.
plays: Immediately review SOXL and SPAL. Both are leveraged, show significant losses, and are either flagged "SELL" or below their stops. Consider exiting or substantially reducing exposure.
Consider a speculative long entry on ZS (cybersecurity) at 153.83-153.59, with a stop at 153.05, supported by the "cyber stock comeback" headline and a strong scanner setup.
